title: TRIzol RNA Extraction from Cultured Cells
author: Thermo Fisher Scientific / Invitrogen

steps.title: Lyse Cells with TRIzol
steps.instruction: Aspirate growth medium from cultured cells (1 well of a 6-well plate, ~1×10^6 cells). Add 1 mL TRIzol Reagent directly to the well. Pipette up and down several times to lyse cells completely. Transfer lysate to a 1.5 mL RNase-free tube.

steps.title: Incubate Lysate
steps.instruction: Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes to permit complete dissociation of nucleoprotein complexes.

steps.title: Add Chloroform and Phase Separation
steps.instruction: Add 200 µL chloroform per 1 mL TRIzol. Cap tube and shake vigorously for 15 seconds. Incubate at room temperature for 3 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge for Phase Separation
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 15 minutes at 4°C. Three layers will separate: the colourless aqueous upper phase (contains RNA), a white interphase, and a red-pink organic lower phase.

steps.title: Transfer Aqueous Phase
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer the aqueous upper phase (~400 µL) to a fresh RNase-free tube. Do NOT disturb the interphase.

steps.title: Precipitate RNA
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL isopropanol. Mix by inversion. Incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes (or -20°C overnight for low-yield samples).

steps.title: Pellet RNA
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 12,000 × g for 10 minutes at 4°C. A white gel-like RNA pellet should be visible at the bottom.

steps.title: Wash RNA Pellet
steps.instruction: Remove and discard supernatant carefully. Wash pellet with 1 mL 75% ethanol (prepared in DEPC-water). Vortex briefly. Centrifuge at 7,500 × g for 5 minutes at 4°C.

steps.title: Air-Dry RNA Pellet
steps.instruction: Remove ethanol completely. Air-dry pellet for 5–10 minutes (do NOT over-dry).

steps.title: Resuspend RNA Pellet
steps.instruction: Resuspend pellet in 20–50 µL RNase-free water. Incubate at 55°C for 10 minutes to dissolve.

steps.title: Quantify and Check RNA Integrity
steps.instruction: Quantify on NanoDrop at A260/280 — expect ratio 1.8–2.1 for pure RNA. Check integrity by running 1 µg on a 1% agarose gel — expect two distinct 28S and 18S rRNA bands.
